title: Using 'Yo' and Verb Forms
description: Learn when to use 'yo' for emphasis and explore verb forms derived from nouns.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: In Spanish, 'quiero' means 'I want' without needing the word for 'I'. The word for 'I' is 'yo', but it's only used for emphasis. How would you say 'I want' with emphasis?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Yo quiero. Now, how would you say 'I don't want'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Yo no quiero. Good. How would you say 'I don't want to cancel'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Yo no quiero cancelar. Now, how do you say 'celebration'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Celebración. And to celebrate?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Celebrar. How would you say 'I want to celebrate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero celebrar. What does 'quiero ilustrar' mean?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Correct. 'Ilustrar' means 'to illustrate'. Now, how would you say 'I want to find out'? In Spanish, you say 'I want to inform myself'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero informarme. How do you say 'information'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Información. Now, how would you say 'I don't want to oblige you'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No quiero obligarte. How would you say 'I want to cancel it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero cancelarlo. Now, let's talk about the Spanish 'r'. An initial 'r' is rolled, like in 'regular'. The word for 'but' is 'pero', and 'dog' is 'perro'. How would you say 'I want to regulate it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero regularlo. Let's move to 'salvation'. How do you say it?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Salvación. And 'to save'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Salvar. How would you say 'I want to save him'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero salvarlo. Now, let's look at 'situation'. How do you say it?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Situación. And 'to situate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Situar. Now, 'participation'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Participación. And 'to participate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Participar. Remember to break words into syllables to avoid mistakes. How would you say 'creation'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Creación. And 'to create'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Crear. Now, 'to experiment' or 'to experience'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Experimentar. This verb means both 'to experiment' and 'to experience'. How do you say 'difference'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Diferencia. And 'influence'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Influencia. How about 'existence'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Existencia. And 'consistence'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Consistencia. Finally, 'inconsistence'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Inconsistencia. Great job!

outro.text: You've learned how to use 'yo' for emphasis and how to form verbs from nouns.
Try forming more verbs from nouns you know. Think of 'communication' or 'operation' and convert them to Spanish.
Remember, breaking words into syllables helps avoid mistakes. Practice this technique with new words.
Next time, we'll explore more verb forms and continue building your Spanish vocabulary.
